title: Meta Rings The Bell—Again
text:
Meta Rings The Bell—Again

Louis-Vincent Gave

Gavekal Research | 7 Jul 2026

Between 2019 and 2022, large US tech companies could not hire employees fast enough. In less than three years, Meta more than doubled its workforce, as did Amazon. Alphabet and Microsoft both expanded their workforces by around 50%. The race was clearly on for engineering talent, account managers, human resources staff and more. By 2022, however, the share prices of the big tech firms had stopped rising. This posed a problem for their business model since much of their top talent is rewarded through share grants and options rather than high wages. Stalling share prices risked prompting high performers to leave for start-ups or competitors, or to remain but with diminished motivation.
